description: FGAP-051 step 1 (user-directed, empirical-first): stop an over-long LLM label/name/title from ABORTING the trio run, via the PROMPT + the existing bounded retry -- NO truncation, model field caps kept (not sacred; relaxing is the contingent step-2 fallback only if the dry-run shows the LLM still overflows). (a) PROMPT: a guarded ai/00NN migration adds to the ~5 capped-field-producing spec templates (A1 narrative-draft title; C1 suggest-feedback-channels label+instrument; D1 propose-milestones label; F5 propose-evidence label; G1 propose-review-loop review-event label + communication channel) a concise, communicative directive naming the REASON -- the value is stored in a 200-char (instrument 500) database column and will fail to save if longer, so keep it concise + communicative, well under the limit. (b) RETRY: the capped-field parsers (planner/specs.py) validate length after the non-empty check -> raise ValueError on overflow (message names the field, its length, and the cap) -> the EXISTING resilience loop (_run_spec_with_resilience, DEFAULT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=2 = 1 initial + 1 retry) re-renders with the rejection as steering. Bounded, not wasteful. On retry-exhaustion the existing per-spec degrade applies (the over-long value never reaches the terminal save), so the run COMPLETES rather than aborting -- a strict improvement over the current whole-run abort; NO truncation. Then DRY-RUN run_trio and observe whether the prompt+retry makes the LLM comply (FGAP-051 stays open until the real dry-run confirms no abort). Addresses FEAT-010 + FGAP-051; harness SLICE=label_length.
acceptance_criteria: The capped-field-producing specs' prompts instruct the LLM, with the reason (the value is stored in a fixed-length DB column and will break on save if longer), to produce a concise, communicative value within the column limit.

An over-long value from a capped-field spec triggers ONE bounded retry-with-feedback (the rejection naming the field, its length, and the cap) via the existing resilience mechanism -- not wasteful, and with NO truncation of the value.

An over-long value no longer ABORTS the run: the length validation + retry keep the over-long value out of the terminal save, so the run completes and yields a saved proposed Plan (worst case, on retry-exhaustion, the offending spec degrades per the existing resilience -- the run still completes; the value is never truncated and the model field caps are unchanged).
The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates; the prompt-template migration applies forward and reverses cleanly (guarded byte-match).
